cells.json_text_content: ASU 2015-02 — Consolidation (Topic 810): Amendments to the Consolidation Analysis
cells.pdf_text_content: ASU 2015-02 — Consolidation (Topic 810): Amendments to the Consolidation Analysis

cells.json_text_content: February 2015
cells.pdf_text_content: February 2015

cells.json_text_content: Certain factors that previously required reporting entities to consolidate a given legal entity have been eliminated, requiring fewer legal entities to be consolidated under the new guidance.
cells.pdf_text_content: Certain factors that previously required reporting entities to consolidate a given legal entity have been eliminated, requiring fewer legal entities to be consolidated under the new guidance.

cells.json_text_content: ASU 2015-05 — Intangibles - Goodwill and Other - Internal Use Software (Subtopic 350-40): Customer's Accounting for Fees Paid in a Cloud Computing Arrangement
cells.pdf_text_content: ASU 2015-05 — Intangibles - Goodwill and Other - Internal Use Software (Subtopic 350-40): Customer's Accounting for Fees Paid in a Cloud Computing Arrangement

cells.json_text_content: April 2015
cells.pdf_text_content: April 2015

cells.json_text_content: An entity that is the customer in a cloud computing arrangement that includes a software license should account for the software license element of the arrangement consistent with the acquisition of other software licenses.
cells.pdf_text_content: An entity that is the customer in a cloud computing arrangement that includes a software license should account for the software license element of the arrangement consistent with the acquisition of other software licenses.

cells.json_text_content: ASU 2015-07 - Fair Value Measurement (Topic 820): Disclosures for Investments in Certain Entities That Calculate Net Asset Value per Share (or its Equivalent) (a consensus of the Emerging Issues Taskforce)
cells.pdf_text_content: ASU 2015-07 - Fair Value Measurement (Topic 820): Disclosures for Investments in Certain Entities That Calculate Net Asset Value per Share (or its Equivalent) (a consensus of the Emerging Issues Taskforce)

cells.json_text_content: May 2015
cells.pdf_text_content: May 2015

cells.json_text_content: Investments for which fair value is measured at net asset value per share (or its equivalent) using the practical expedient of ASC 820 should not be categorized in the fair value hierarchy. However, the reporting entity should continue to disclose information on such investments.
cells.pdf_text_content: Investments for which fair value is measured at net asset value per share (or its equivalent) using the practical expedient of ASC 820 should not be categorized in the fair value hierarchy. However, the reporting entity should continue to disclose information on such investments.

cells.json_text_content: ASU 2016-05 - Effect of Derivative Contract Novations on Existing Hedge Accounting Relationships
cells.pdf_text_content: ASU 2016-05 - Effect of Derivative Contract Novations on Existing Hedge Accounting Relationships

cells.json_text_content: March 2016
cells.pdf_text_content: March 2016

cells.json_text_content: The amendment clarifies language related to hedge accounting criteria that a change in the counterparty is not in and of itself considered a termination of the derivative or critical term of the hedging relationship.
cells.pdf_text_content: The amendment clarifies language related to hedge accounting criteria that a change in the counterparty is not in and of itself considered a termination of the derivative or critical term of the hedging relationship.

cells.json_text_content: January 2016
cells.pdf_text_content: January 2016

cells.json_text_content: The amendment revises accounting related to the classification and measurement of investments in equity securities and the presentation of certain fair value changes for financial liabilities measured at fair value. The ASU also amends certain disclosure requirements associated with the fair value of financial instruments.
cells.pdf_text_content: The amendment revises accounting related to the classification and measurement of investments in equity securities and the presentation of certain fair value changes for financial liabilities measured at fair value. The ASU also amends certain disclosure requirements associated with the fair value of financial instruments.

cells.pdf_text_content: ASU 2015-11 - Inventory (Topic 330): Simplifying the Measurement of Inventory

cells.json_text_content: July 2015
cells.pdf_text_content: July 2015

cells.json_text_content: The amendment requires entities to measure inventory under the FIFO or average cost methods at the lower of cost or net realizable value.
cells.pdf_text_content: The amendment requires entities to measure inventory under the FIFO or average cost methods at the lower of cost or net realizable value.

cells.json_text_content: Revenue Recognition ASUs:2014-09 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers2015-14 - Deferral of the Effective Date2016-08 - Principal Versus Agent Considerations2016-10 - Identifying Performance Obligations and Licensing2016-11 - Revenue Recognition and Derivatives and Hedging2016-12 - Narrow-Scope Improvements & Practical Expedients2016-20--Technical Corrections and Improvements
cells.pdf_text_content: Revenue Recognition ASUs: 2014-09 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers 2015-14 - Deferral of the Effective Date 2016-08 - Principal Versus Agent Considerations 2016-10 - Identifying Performance Obligations and Licensing 2016-11 - Revenue Recognition and Derivatives and Hedging 2016-12 - Narrow-Scope Improvements & Practical Expedients 2016-20--Technical Corrections and Improvements

cells.json_text_content: Various
cells.pdf_text_content: Various

cells.json_text_content: Recognition standard contains principles for entities to apply to determine the measurement of revenue and timing of when the revenue is recognized. The underlying principle of the updated guidance will have entities recognize revenue to depict the transfer of goods or services to customers at an amount that is expected to be received in exchange for those goods or services.
cells.pdf_text_content: Recognition standard contains principles for entities to apply to determine the measurement of revenue and timing of when the revenue is recognized. The underlying principle of the updated guidance will have entities recognize revenue to depict the transfer of goods or services to customers at an amount that is expected to be received in exchange for those goods or services.
